enonce.texte: Read the sentences below and identify the conjunctions. Then, classify them as coordinating (and, but, or, so, for, nor, yet) or subordinating (although, because, if, when, while, etc.).

questions.texte: Identify and classify the conjunctions in the following sentences.

sousQuestions.texte: I like rice and beans.
sousQuestions.reponse: and is a coordinating conjunction.

sousQuestions.texte: She stayed at home because she was sick.
sousQuestions.reponse: because is a subordinating conjunction.

sousQuestions.texte: He wanted to go, but he was too tired.
sousQuestions.reponse: but is a coordinating conjunction.

sousQuestions.texte: You will pass the exam if you study hard.
sousQuestions.reponse: if is a subordinating conjunction.

sousQuestions.texte: Although it was raining, they went to the market.
sousQuestions.reponse: although is a subordinating conjunction.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct coordinating conjunction: and, but, or, so, for, nor, yet.

questions.texte: Choose the correct conjunction to complete each sentence.

sousQuestions.texte: She studied hard, ______ she passed the exam.
sousQuestions.reponse: so (result)

sousQuestions.texte: I wanted to go to the party, ______ I was too tired.
sousQuestions.reponse: but (contrast)

sousQuestions.texte: He likes to read ______ write stories.
sousQuestions.reponse: and (addition)

sousQuestions.texte: You can have tea ______ coffee.
sousQuestions.reponse: or (choice)

sousQuestions.texte: He must be tired, ______ he has been working all day.
sousQuestions.reponse: for (reason)

enonce.texte: Combine the following pairs of sentences using the subordinating conjunction given in brackets.

questions.texte: Combine the sentences using the correct subordinating conjunction.
questions.reponse:
1. Although it was raining, we went to school. / We went to school although it was raining.
2. She was happy because she got a good grade.
3. If you work hard, you will succeed.
4. He was reading while she was cooking.
5. Because he was late, he missed the bus.

sousQuestions.texte: It was raining. We went to school. (although)
sousQuestions.reponse: Although it was raining, we went to school. OR We went to school although it was raining.

sousQuestions.texte: She got a good grade. She was happy. (because)
sousQuestions.reponse: She was happy because she got a good grade.

sousQuestions.texte: You work hard. You will succeed. (if)
sousQuestions.reponse: If you work hard, you will succeed.

sousQuestions.texte: He was reading. She was cooking. (while)
sousQuestions.reponse: He was reading while she was cooking.

sousQuestions.texte: He was late. He missed the bus. (because)
sousQuestions.reponse: Because he was late, he missed the bus.

intitule: Expressing Purpose - so as to / in order to

enonce.texte: Rewrite the sentences using so as to or in order to to express purpose.

questions.texte: Rewrite each sentence to express purpose.
questions.reponse:
1. She studied hard in order to pass the exam. / She studied hard so as to pass the exam.
2. He woke up early in order to catch the bus. / He woke up early so as to catch the bus.
3. They went to the market in order to buy food. / They went to the market so as to buy food.
4. He exercises every day in order to stay healthy. / He exercises every day so as to stay healthy.
5. She saved money in order to buy a new phone. / She saved money so as to buy a new phone.

sousQuestions.texte: She studied hard. She wanted to pass the exam.
sousQuestions.reponse: She studied hard in order to pass the exam. OR She studied hard so as to pass the exam.

sousQuestions.texte: He woke up early. He wanted to catch the bus.
sousQuestions.reponse: He woke up early in order to catch the bus. OR He woke up early so as to catch the bus.

sousQuestions.texte: They went to the market. They wanted to buy food.
sousQuestions.reponse: They went to the market in order to buy food. OR They went to the market so as to buy food.

sousQuestions.texte: He exercises every day. He wants to stay healthy.
sousQuestions.reponse: He exercises every day in order to stay healthy. OR He exercises every day so as to stay healthy.

sousQuestions.texte: She saved money. She wanted to buy a new phone.
sousQuestions.reponse: She saved money in order to buy a new phone. OR She saved money so as to buy a new phone.

intitule: Negative Purpose - so as not to / in order not to

enonce.texte: Rewrite the sentences using so as not to or in order not to to express negative purpose.

questions.texte: Rewrite each sentence to express negative purpose.
questions.reponse:
1. He left early in order not to miss the bus. / He left early so as not to miss the bus.
2. She spoke quietly in order not to wake the baby. / She spoke quietly so as not to wake the baby.
3. He put on his mask in order not to get sick. / He put on his mask so as not to get sick.
4. She locked the door in order not to be disturbed. / She locked the door so as not to be disturbed.
5. He worked hard in order not to fail the exam. / He worked hard so as not to fail the exam.

sousQuestions.texte: He left early. He didn't want to miss the bus.
sousQuestions.reponse: He left early in order not to miss the bus. OR He left early so as not to miss the bus.

sousQuestions.texte: She spoke quietly. She didn't want to wake the baby.
sousQuestions.reponse: She spoke quietly in order not to wake the baby. OR She spoke quietly so as not to wake the baby.

sousQuestions.texte: He put on his mask. He didn't want to get sick.
sousQuestions.reponse: He put on his mask in order not to get sick. OR He put on his mask so as not to get sick.

sousQuestions.texte: She locked the door. She didn't want to be disturbed.
sousQuestions.reponse: She locked the door in order not to be disturbed. OR She locked the door so as not to be disturbed.

sousQuestions.texte: He worked hard. He didn't want to fail the exam.
sousQuestions.reponse: He worked hard in order not to fail the exam. OR He worked hard so as not to fail the exam.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the sentences using unless or otherwise as indicated.

questions.reponse:
1. Unless you study, you will not pass the exam.
2. Study hard; otherwise, you will not pass the exam.
3. Unless he hurries, he will miss the bus.
4. Hurry up; otherwise, you will be late.
5. Unless it stops raining, we will not go out.

sousQuestions.texte: If you do not study, you will not pass the exam. (unless)
sousQuestions.reponse: Unless you study, you will not pass the exam.

sousQuestions.texte: You must study hard. If you don't, you will not pass the exam. (otherwise)
sousQuestions.reponse: Study hard; otherwise, you will not pass the exam.

sousQuestions.texte: If he does not hurry, he will miss the bus. (unless)
sousQuestions.reponse: Unless he hurries, he will miss the bus.

sousQuestions.texte: Hurry up! If you don't, you will be late. (otherwise)
sousQuestions.reponse: Hurry up; otherwise, you will be late.

sousQuestions.texte: If it does not stop raining, we will not go out. (unless)
sousQuestions.reponse: Unless it stops raining, we will not go out.

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the appropriate subordinating conjunction: although, because, if, when, while, unless.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct subordinating conjunction.

sousQuestions.texte: ______ he was tired, he continued working.
sousQuestions.reponse: Although (contrast)

sousQuestions.texte: She went to the hospital ______ she was feeling sick.
sousQuestions.reponse: because (reason)

sousQuestions.texte: ______ you finish your homework, you can't go out.
sousQuestions.reponse: Unless (condition)

sousQuestions.texte: Call me ______ you arrive at the station.
sousQuestions.reponse: when (time)

sousQuestions.texte: ______ she was reading, he was watching TV.
sousQuestions.reponse: While (simultaneous actions)

enonce.texte:
Write a well-structured essay of about 200 words on the following topic:

'The advantages of learning English for Cameroonian students.'

In your essay, you must use at least:

  • 2 coordinating conjunctions (and, but, or, so, for, nor, yet)
  • 3 subordinating conjunctions (although, because, if, when, while, etc.)
  • 1 expression of purpose (in order to / so as to)

questions.texte: Write an essay on the advantages of learning English for Cameroonian students.
questions.reponse: Check the essay for correct use of conjunctions, logical structure (introduction, body, conclusion), appropriate vocabulary, and correct grammar.

sousQuestions.texte: Plan your essay: Write an introduction, 2-3 body paragraphs, and a conclusion.
sousQuestions.reponse: Introduction: Introduce the topic and state your main argument. Body: Present advantages (career opportunities, access to information, international communication). Conclusion: Summarize and give a final opinion.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify and highlight the conjunctions and purpose expressions you used in your essay.
sousQuestions.reponse: Check for: and, but, or, so, for, nor, yet (coordinating); although, because, if, when, while (subordinating); in order to / so as to (purpose).

enonce.texte:
Write a dialogue between a customer and a trader in a market. The customer wants to buy items for a school project. Use appropriate vocabulary and conjunctions in your dialogue.

Your dialogue should include:

  • At least 5 exchanges
  • 2 coordinating conjunctions
  • 2 subordinating conjunctions
  • Polite expressions (good morning, please, thank you, etc.)

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between a customer and a trader in a market.
questions.reponse: Check for correct dialogue format, appropriate vocabulary, correct use of conjunctions, and politeness expressions.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the conjunctions you used in your dialogue.
sousQuestions.reponse: Check for: and, but, or, so, for, nor, yet (coordinating); although, because, if, when, while (subordinating).

enonce.texte: Transform the following sentences by changing the conjunction as indicated while keeping the same meaning.

questions.texte: Rewrite the sentences using the conjunction in brackets.
questions.reponse:
1. I stayed at home because it was raining. (so) → It was raining, so I stayed at home.
2. Although he was tired, he finished the work. (but) → He was tired, but he finished the work.
3. If you don't study, you will fail. (unless) → Unless you study, you will fail.
4. She went to the market to buy food. (in order to) → She went to the market in order to buy food.
5. He is rich yet unhappy. (although) → Although he is rich, he is unhappy.

sousQuestions.texte: I stayed at home because it was raining. (so)
sousQuestions.reponse: It was raining, so I stayed at home.

sousQuestions.texte: Although he was tired, he finished the work. (but)
sousQuestions.reponse: He was tired, but he finished the work.

sousQuestions.texte: If you don't study, you will fail. (unless)
sousQuestions.reponse: Unless you study, you will fail.

sousQuestions.texte: She went to the market to buy food. (in order to)
sousQuestions.reponse: She went to the market in order to buy food.

sousQuestions.texte: He is rich yet unhappy. (although)
sousQuestions.reponse: Although he is rich, he is unhappy.
